{"key_event":"Gaitán assassination and the Bogotazo","reasoning":"WWII-linked industrialization, labor growth, mass media, and Gaitanista mobilization enlarged urban civil society, while reforms and U.S. alignment modestly deepened state capacity. But partisan polarization, rural armed fragmentation, the politicization of coercion, and the 1948 Bogotazo left both society and state less able to sustain order or accountability than in 1938.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":6.1,"state_change":-0.7,"state_power":6.4}
